aboutText: Gaston Ibarroule is a sound designer and music composer from Neuquén, Argentina, based in Berlin, Germany, since 2015. His career in the world of sound began through music, playing guitar and singing from an early age. Driven by a broad interest in the field, he has developed in different areas including sound design, music composition and production, field recording, dialogue and effects editing and mixing. His body of work spans short and feature-length fiction films, documentaries, experimental films, as well as television, streaming and advertising.
homeIntro: Sound designer & Composer from Argentina, based in Berlin. My work spans film, series, documentary, art installations, TV & ads.

faqs.question: What audio formats does Sonidata support for recording?
faqs.answer: Sonidata records in three quality tiers: Pro (WAV 48 kHz / 24-bit) for professional delivery and post-production, High (WAV 44.1 kHz / 16-bit) for general-purpose capture, and Compressed (M4A AAC) for extended sessions where storage is a priority. Pro quality is available with a Pro subscription.

faqs.question: How does UCS integration work?
faqs.answer: Sonidata fully implements the Universal Category System (UCS v8.2). You assign Category, Subcategory, CatID, FX Name, Creator ID, Source ID, and Location via the Naming View; the app then auto-generates a UCS-compliant filename (e.g., ANMLDog_Bark-Close_GI_MKH8060_Park.wav) and can organize files into a matching folder hierarchy automatically.

faqs.question: Can I control recording with Siri or the Action Button?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sonidata exposes Start Recording, Stop Recording, and Toggle Recording actions to the Shortcuts app. You can trigger them from Siri, Back Tap, the Action Button (iPhone 15 Pro+), or any automation flow. The sonidata:// URL scheme is also available for third-party tools.

faqs.question: What is the Live Activity on my Lock Screen?
faqs.answer: When a recording is running, a Live Activity appears on your Lock Screen and Dynamic Island showing a live timer, the recording name, and a red dot indicator. It starts automatically when you begin recording and disappears when you stop — no configuration needed.

faqs.question: How do FX Name Presets speed up my workflow?
faqs.answer: Presets let you save reusable FX Name templates. Swipe right on the FX Name row to apply a saved template — including a "Last Used" shortcut. Templates support smart placeholders like <Location, State> (auto-filled from GPS) and a Time of Day button that appends the current solar period (Dawn, Morning, Day, Evening, Dusk, Night).

faqs.question: What is the Metadata Clipboard?
faqs.answer: The Metadata Clipboard lets you prepare metadata while a recording is still running. Fill in your fields, tap Copy, then after stopping, open the new file and tap Paste to apply everything in one step. It's ideal for back-to-back field sessions where category and location stay the same.

faqs.question: Does Sonidata embed artwork into audio files?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you attach a photo, it's embedded directly into the audio file — as an ID3v2 APIC tag in WAV files and as iTunes metadata in M4A files. This means the artwork travels with the file and is visible in Finder, Soundminer, AirDrop previews, and other tools without needing a separate image.

faqs.question: Is the CSV export compatible with Soundminer?
faqs.answer: The CSV headers are aligned with Soundminer V5 field names — including FXName, Designer, Manufacturer, Microphone, CategoryFull, Channels, and more — so you can import directly into Soundminer without manual column remapping.

faqs.question: Can I import audio from Voice Memos or other apps?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Use the iOS Share button in any app (Files, Voice Memos, etc.) and select Sonidata. The file is copied to a shared container and automatically moved into your Documents folder the next time you open the app — ready for UCS tagging.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Free and Pro?
faqs.answer: Free users can record in High and Compressed quality, tag files with UCS metadata, use SmartSearch, FX Name Presets, Batch Delete, Siri Shortcuts, and the Live Activity. Pro unlocks WAV 48/24 recording, Voice Slate, Smart Location, Metadata Clipboard, Photo Attachments, Embedded Artwork, Folder Browser, Calendar Filter, Map Viewer, Batch Rename, Visual Date Export, and ZIP Export. Pro is a one-time lifetime purchase — no subscription.

faqs.question: What is Sonidata Embed?
faqs.answer: Sonidata Embed is a free desktop companion app for macOS. It takes the ZIP exports from your iOS device and automatically embeds the metadata (BEXT, iXML, CUE markers, and artwork) directly into your original recorder's WAV files so they can be read natively by Soundminer, Pro Tools, and Reaper.

content.text: Audio Settings
Sonidata gives you full control over your recording format with three independent settings in **Settings → Audio**:
Unsupported combinations are automatically hidden based on your device and connected microphone hardware capabilities.
WAV format, high sample rates, and 24/32-bit depth are Pro features. Free users can record in M4A at 44.1 kHz.
Input Gain
Adjust recording level in **Settings → Audio → Input Gain**:

items: dB scale from -∞ to +12 dB with 0.5 dB steps
Double-tap the slider to reset to 0 dB
Uses hardware gain for external interfaces; falls back to digital (vDSP) gain for the built-in microphone
Caption indicates whether hardware or digital mode is active

content.text: Record Ready / Monitor Mode
Optional pre-record monitoring that lets you check audio levels before committing to a recording:

items: Enable in **Settings → Audio → Record Ready**
The center button now has a **3-state flow**: idle → monitor → record → stop
In monitor mode, a gradient background (green → yellow → red) acts as a visual level meter
Choose between VU and Peak ballistics in Settings
The tab bar shows an **amber indicator** while monitoring

content.text: No audio file is created during monitoring — it uses a zero-file metering tap on the audio engine.
How to Record

items: Tap the **center record button** (red circle) on the bottom tab bar.
A **recording panel** appears at the bottom with a live waveform, timer, and format badge.
Tap the **stop button** to finish recording.
The **Naming View** automatically opens so you can tag the recording immediately.

content.text: Recording Indicator
While recording, the **record button pulses with a red glow** on the Naming and Settings tabs — a subtle reminder that a session is active even when you're working in other parts of the app.
Live Activity (Lock Screen) *(New in Build 13)*
When a recording is in progress, a **Live Activity** appears on your Lock Screen and Dynamic Island showing:

items: A **live timer** with the current recording duration.
The **recording name** being captured.
A **red dot** indicator so you never forget a session is running.

content.text: The Live Activity starts automatically when you begin recording and disappears when you stop — no setup required.
Stereo Recording Modes *(New in Build 14)*
Sonidata supports four recording modes, selectable in **Settings → Recording → Stereo Mode**:

rows: Two identical channels from a single source
Native stereo capture using the built-in mic's stereo polar pattern
Mid-Side decoding from a stereo M/S microphone

items: **True Stereo** with the built-in mic automatically configures the stereo polar pattern and portrait input orientation.
**M/S Stereo** uses the real-time DSP engine to decode Mid and Side signals into a stereo file.
Per-microphone settings (mode, polar pattern, stereo width) are saved and restored automatically when you reconnect a mic.

content.text: Stereo Recording (True Stereo & M/S) is a Pro feature. Free users can record in Mono and Dual Mono.
Recording Tips

items: The app records in the background — you can switch to other apps without stopping.
The waveform display shows real-time audio levels so you can monitor your input.
A **waveform-style stereo meter** overlay shows levels from -60 to +6 dB with green/yellow/red zones, peak hold indicators, and 60Hz refresh rate. The meter persists when navigating between tabs.
Files are saved to the app's Documents folder with a default name like `New Recording 2026-02-28_14-30-00.wav`.
New recordings are **instantly visible** in your library — no need to wait for a file scan.
You can rename them immediately using the Naming View, or tag them later from the file list.

content.text: Audio Device Selection
Independent control over recording input and playback output in **Settings → Audio → Audio Routing**:

items: **Input Device**: Select any available microphone (built-in, USB, Bluetooth). Your selection is saved by device UID and automatically restored when reconnected.
**Output Routing**: Choose between Automatic, Speaker Preferred, or Bluetooth A2DP
Hardware capabilities (sample rate, bit depth, stereo) are re-evaluated per selected input device

content.text: The Naming View is the core of Sonidata. It's where you assign UCS-compliant metadata to your recordings.
What is UCS?

The **Universal Category System (UCS v8.2)** is the industry standard for categorizing and naming sound effects. It ensures your files are named consistently and can be imported into any sound library management software (BaseHead, Soundly, etc.).
**UCS Filename Format:**
{CatID}{UserCategory}_{VendorCategory}{FXName}_{CreatorID}_{SourceID}_{Location-UserData}
**Example:** `ANMLDog_Bark-Close_GI_MKH8060_Park.wav`
Metadata Fields

rows: Longer text description of the recording (separate from FX Name)
CU, MCU, MS, Wide, Distant, INT, EXT

content.text: How to Tag a Recording

items: **Search for a category**: Use the search bar at the top. Sonidata uses **SmartSearch** powered by Apple's NLEmbedding (NaturalLanguage framework) for semantic search — type "dog" and it'll find ANIMALS > DOG even if you don't know the exact category name.
**Select from the picker**: The dual-wheel picker shows Category on the left and Subcategory on the right.
**Fill in the FX Name**: Type the descriptive name, or use **Voice Slate** to dictate it.
**Add Creator ID and Source ID**: These identify you and your equipment. Enable "Remember IDs" in Settings to save these across sessions.
**Add Location**: Tap the location button to auto-fill from GPS, or type manually.
**Attach a Photo**: Tap the camera icon to take a photo or choose from your library.
**Preview the filename**: The bottom bar shows a live preview of the final UCS filename.
**Tap "Rename File"**: The audio file is renamed, metadata is saved to the database, and the file is optionally organized into a UCS folder.

content.text: FX Name Presets *(New in v0.5.0 Build 12)*
Speed up your naming workflow with reusable FX Name templates:

items: **Quick access**: Swipe right on the FX Name row to see your saved templates, including a "Last Used" option.
**Custom templates**: Go to **Settings → FX Name Presets** to create, edit, and manage your own naming templates.
**Smart placeholders**: Use `<Location, State>` in a template and it will be auto-filled with your actual GPS location when applied.
**Time of Day**: Tap the ☀️ button to append the current solar period (Dawn, Morning, Day, Evening, Dusk, Night) to your FX Name — localized in all 21 languages.
**Voice Memos mode**: Recordings are auto-named using your GPS location (e.g., `Berlin 001.wav`) so nothing stays unnamed.

content.text: SmartSearch
The category search is not just text matching — it uses **Apple's Natural Language framework** to understand what you mean:

items: **Semantic search**: Type "rain" and it finds WEATHER > RAIN, but also related categories.
**Synonym matching**: The UCS database includes synonyms, so "puppy" finds ANIMALS > DOG.
**Weighted scoring**: Exact category matches score highest (×20), followed by subcategory, synonyms, explanations, and semantic neighbors.

content.text: Metadata Clipboard (Copy / Paste) *(New in Build 13)*
Prepare metadata while recording and apply it to the finished file in one tap:

items: **During recording**, fill in Category, FX Name, Creator ID, and any other fields you want.
Tap the **Copy** button to save the entire metadata state.
**After stopping**, open the Naming View for the new recording.
Tap **Paste** — all fields are populated instantly.

content.text: This is perfect for field sessions where you know the category and location before you hit record. No more retyping the same data file after file.
The Paste button only appears when there is metadata in the clipboard.
Batch Rename
You can rename **multiple files at once**:

items: In the file list, enter **Edit Mode** (tap "Edit" or long-press).
Select multiple files.
Tap "Rename" — the Naming View opens in batch mode.
All selected files receive the same metadata and are renamed sequentially.

content.text: The batch rename adds a sequential index to each file's FX Name to keep them unique.
Batch Delete *(New in Build 13)*
Quickly remove multiple recordings at once:

items: Enter **Edit Mode** and select the files you want to remove.
Tap the **red trash button** in the batch action bar.
Confirm in the alert — all selected files are moved to trash.

content.text: Batch delete works everywhere: All Files, Folder Contents, and the Record View. It's **free for all users** — no Pro subscription required.
Voice Slate lets you **dictate the FX Name** using speech-to-text instead of typing.
How to Use

items: In the Naming View, tap the **microphone icon** next to the FX Name field.
Speak clearly — describe the sound (e.g., "Bark Close").
The transcription appears in the FX Name field.

content.text: Technical Details

items: Uses Apple's native **`SFSpeechRecognizer`** — all processing happens on-device, completely private.
Injects dynamic contextual hints from UCS categories to improve accuracy for sound design terminology.
Records to a temporary PCM 16kHz mono file, then transcribes.

content.text: Voice Slate Language
You can change the language for Voice Slate recognition: **Settings → Recording → Voice Slate Language**
Options include Device Default and all languages supported by Apple's Speech framework.
Voice Slate is free for all users.
Control Sonidata hands-free with **Siri, the Shortcuts app, Back Tap, or the Action Button**.
Available Actions

rows: Starts or stops depending on current state

content.text: How to Set Up

items: Open the **Shortcuts** app on your iPhone.
Create a new shortcut and search for **Sonidata**.
Choose an action (Start, Stop, or Toggle).
Assign the shortcut to a trigger: Siri voice command, **Back Tap** (double or triple tap the back of your phone), the **Action Button** (iPhone 15 Pro and later), or a home screen widget.

content.text: Deep Linking
Sonidata also supports the `sonidata://` URL scheme for integration with third-party automation tools.
Assign "Toggle Recording" to Back Tap for instant one-handed recording — no need to open the app.
Sonidata can automatically tag recordings with GPS location data.
How It Works

items: Grant location permission when prompted (or enable it in Settings → Privacy → Location).
When you open the Naming View, the current location is automatically detected.
Tap the **location button** to auto-fill the Location field.
The full address, coordinates (latitude/longitude), and short address are all saved with the recording.

content.text: Location Accuracy

items: **"When In Use"** permission: Location is fetched when you open the app.
**"Always"** permission: Location is cached and refreshed every 5 minutes in the background, so metadata is ready instantly when you tag.
Reverse geocoding converts GPS coordinates to a human-readable address.

content.text: What Gets Stored

items: **Short location** (used in filename): e.g., "Berlin"
**Full address**: e.g., "Sonnenallee 45, 12045 Berlin, Germany"
**Coordinates**: Latitude and longitude (shown on the Map Viewer)

content.text: Enable "Always" location access for the fastest metadata loading. Sonidata will pre-fetch your location as soon as the app becomes active — no waiting.
Smart Location is free for all users.
Sonidata offers **four ways** to browse your recordings:
1. All Files (List View)
A flat list of all recordings, with:

items: **Expandable rows**: Tap any file to see its location, photo, and an inline audio player with waveform.
**Vendor Category badge**: When a Vendor Category is set, it's displayed directly on the file row for quick reference.
**Search**: Filter by filename.
**Sort**: By name, date, or category.
**Filter pills**: Filter by category, subcategory, or location.
**Swipe actions**: Swipe left for Edit/Share/Delete, swipe right to Select.

content.text: 2. Folder Browser
A grid view of your UCS folders:

items: Shows folder name and file count.
Tap a folder to see its contents.
Same swipe actions and expandable rows as the list view.

content.text: #### Calendar Filter Bar *(New in Build 13)*
Filter your folder grid by **recording date** using the horizontal filter bar at the top:

items: Tap a **Year** pill to narrow the view to folders containing recordings from that year.
Drill into a **Month**, then a specific **Day**.
The folder grid and file counts update in real-time to show only matching recordings.

content.text: This is a fast way to find recordings from a specific session without scrolling through your entire library.
3. Map Viewer
An interactive map showing all geotagged recordings:

items: Each pin represents a recording with GPS coordinates.
Filter by category or location.
Tap a pin for inline playback.

content.text: View Mode Toggle
Switch between views using the segmented control at the top of the screen. Your preference is saved between sessions.
Sonidata can automatically organize your recordings into a UCS folder structure.
Organization Modes

rows: All files stay in Documents root

content.text: How to Configure
**Settings → Folder Organization**

items: **Auto-Organize**: When enabled, files are moved to their UCS folder immediately after renaming.
**Generate Folders**: Creates the complete UCS folder tree in your Documents directory (based on all categories in the UCS database).
**Organize All**: Batch-organizes all existing recordings into folders based on their current metadata.

content.text: You can attach a photo to any recording — useful for documenting the recording environment, location, or source.
How to Attach

items: In the Naming View, tap the **camera icon** at the top of the screen.
Choose **"Take Photo"** or **"Choose from Library"** from the compact dropdown menu.
The photo is saved to `Documents/Photos/` with a unique ID linking it to the recording.

content.text: Photos are automatically compressed to save storage while preserving quality. They are included in ZIP exports alongside the audio files.
Embedded Artwork *(New in v0.5.0 Build 12)*
Attached photos are now **automatically embedded** into your audio files:

items: **WAV files**: Artwork embedded as ID3v2 APIC tag.
**M4A files**: Artwork embedded as iTunes metadata.

content.text: This means your artwork **travels with the audio file** — visible in Finder, your sound library manager, AirDrop previews, and other apps without needing a separate photo file.
Photo Attachments is a Pro feature.
CSV Export
Export your entire recordings database as a CSV file for import into sound library managers:

items: **Includes**: All UCS metadata fields, location data, coordinates, file paths, audio format, file size, duration, sample rate, bit depth.

**Industry-standard format**: CSV headers are aligned with common sound library management software field names for seamless import. Includes FXName, Designer, Manufacturer, Microphone, CategoryFull, Channels, and more.
**Access**: Settings → Export → Export CSV

content.text: ZIP Export
Export filtered subsets of your library as complete archives:

items: Open the **Export Filter Sheet** from Settings or the file browser.
Set filters: category, subcategory, location, creator ID, date range.
Preview how many files match.
Export as ZIP: includes audio files + photos + CSV metadata.
Share via AirDrop, Files, email, or any share destination.

content.text: ZIP Export is a Pro feature.
Visual Date Export *(New in Build 13)*
Export recordings organized by date:

items: In the **Export Filter Sheet**, use the **calendar date picker** to select specific Year → Month → Day ranges.
Enable **"Group by Date"** to create date-named subfolders inside the ZIP (e.g., `2026-03-05/`).
Each subfolder contains only the recordings from that day, keeping your archive neatly organized by session date.

content.text: Library Statistics
The Export screen shows your library stats at a glance: total recordings, categories used, and file counts.
# Sonidata Embed Documentation
**Version**: 1.3.2 **Last Updated**: 2026-04-22 **Platforms**: macOS (Universal: Intel + Apple Silicon) · Windows **Contact**: sonidata.info@gmail.com
**Embed field recording metadata from Sonidata into your WAV files.**
[Download for macOS](https://gastibarroule.com/sonidata-support) | [Download for Windows](https://gastibarroule.com/sonidata-support)

Sonidata Embed is a companion desktop app for the [Sonidata](https://gastibarroule.com/sonidata-support) iOS field recording toolkit. It takes session exports (ZIP or CSV) and embeds rich metadata directly into WAV files using industry-standard audio metadata formats — ready for your DAW and sound library management software.
Sync your recording sessions between Sonidata iOS and Sonidata Embed desktop via your personal cloud storage:
Supported Providers
How It Works

items: Go to **Settings → Integrations → Cloud Sync**
Enable the provider(s) you want to use
Sign in to your cloud account when prompted
Sonidata syncs session manifests (metadata, photos, folder structure) to your cloud storage
Open Sonidata Embed on your desktop — your sessions appear automatically via the Cloud Sync panel

content.text: What Gets Synced

items: Session metadata (CSV)
Attached photos
Folder structure and organization
Marker data

content.text: **Privacy**: All data goes directly to YOUR personal cloud account. Sonidata has no access to your cloud storage or its contents.
You can import external audio files into Sonidata using the **iOS Share Extension**.
Supported Formats
`.wav`, `.m4a`, `.mp3`, `.aiff`, `.aif`, `.caf`
How to Import

items: In any app (Files, Voice Memos, etc.), tap **Share**.
Select **Sonidata** from the share sheet.
The file is copied to Sonidata's shared container.
Next time you open Sonidata, the file is automatically moved to your Documents folder.
It appears in your recordings list, ready for tagging.

content.text: Voice Memos files are fully supported — share directly from the Voice Memos app into Sonidata for UCS tagging.
Audio

items: **Format**: M4A (AAC) or WAV
**Sample Rate**: 44.1 kHz, 48 kHz, 96 kHz, or 192 kHz
**Bit Depth**: 16-bit, 24-bit, or 32-bit float
**Input Gain**: -∞ to +12 dB with 0.5 dB steps
**Record Ready**: Enable pre-record monitoring mode
**Meter Ballistics**: VU or Peak
**Stereo Mode**: Mono, Dual Mono, True Stereo, or M/S Stereo
**Audio Routing**: Input device and output routing selection
**Voice Slate Language**: Language for speech recognition

content.text: Naming

items: **Auto Title Case**: Automatically capitalize each word in the FX Name field
**Strip Commas**: Remove commas from the FX Name (for CSV-safe filenames)
**Remember IDs**: Persist Creator ID and Source ID between sessions

content.text: File Organization

items: **Organization Mode**: Disabled / Category Only / Full
**Auto-Organize**: Automatically move files to folders after renaming

content.text: Display

items: **Theme**: System / Light / Dark
**Category Descriptions**: Show or hide UCS category explanations in the picker
**Per-Field Filename Toggles**: Choose which UserData fields (Location, Microphone, Mic Perspective, INT/EXT, Single-Use Terms, UID) appear in the filename

content.text: Permissions

items: **Location Access**: Not Determined / When In Use / Always
**Location Precision**: Precise (GPS) or General Area (~1 km)
**Speech Recognition**: Required for Voice Slate

content.text: Integrations

items: **Cloud Sync**: iCloud Drive, Google Drive, Dropbox per-provider toggles
**Desktop App Unlock**: Generate pairing code for Sonidata Embed

content.text: Export

items: **Export CSV**: Export your full database as a spreadsheet (29 columns)
**Export ZIP**: Filtered archive with audio, photos, and CSV
**Group by Date**: Create date-named subfolders in ZIP exports

content.text: About

items: **Version**: Current app and build number
**Support**: Links to website and email
**Privacy Policy**: Link to privacy policy

content.text: **Pro Lifetime**: One-time purchase — no subscription.

items: **All data stays on your device.** Sonidata does not upload recordings or metadata to any server.
**Voice Slate** uses Apple's on-device speech recognition — audio never leaves your device.
**Location data** is stored locally and embedded in the file metadata. It is not shared with anyone.
**Cloud backups** go to YOUR personal iCloud/Google Drive account — Sonidata has no access to your cloud storage.
**No analytics, no tracking, no ads.**

content.text: Recordings don't appear in the list

items: Recordings now appear **instantly** after stopping — if one is missing, re-open the app to trigger a filesystem rescan.
Check that the recording is in the app's Documents folder (not a system folder).
If the file was moved manually via Files app, Sonidata will re-sync on next launch.

content.text: Voice Slate isn't working

items: Check that Speech Recognition permission is granted: **iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Speech Recognition**.
Ensure you have a stable internet connection for the first download of the speech model (subsequent uses are offline).
Try changing the Voice Slate Language in Settings if accuracy is poor.

content.text: Location isn't auto-filling

items: Check permission: **iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Location → "Always"** (recommended) or **"While Using"**.
If using "While Using", the location may take a moment to fetch when you first open the Naming View.
GPS accuracy is lower indoors — step outside for a more precise reading.

content.text: Files aren't organizing into folders

items: Check that Folder Organization is enabled: **Settings → Folder Organization → Mode** (set to Category or Full).
Check that Auto-Organize is turned on.
If files were recorded before enabling organization, use "Organize All" to batch-process them.

content.text: Siri Shortcuts don't trigger recording

items: Make sure Sonidata has been opened at least once after the update so the App Intents are registered.
Check **Settings → Siri & Search** and confirm Sonidata shortcuts are visible.
If using Back Tap, verify it's configured: **Settings → Accessibility → Touch → Back Tap**.

content.text: Live Activity not showing on Lock Screen

items: Live Activities must be enabled: **iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Live Activities**.
Ensure you're running iOS 16.1 or later (required for ActivityKit).

content.text: Export ZIP is empty

items: Check that your filters actually match some recordings (the preview count shows how many files will be included).
Make sure the recordings have audio files on disk (missing files are shown with a "missing" indicator).

content.text: Importing from Voice Memos doesn't work

items: Make sure you're using the **Share** button in Voice Memos, not drag-and-drop.
If the import seems stuck, close and re-open Sonidata — the file will be picked up automatically.

content.text: Theme looks wrong or colors are stuck

items: Switch themes in **Settings → Display → Theme**.
If using System, the app follows your iPhone's appearance setting (Settings → Display & Brightness).

content.text: Sonidata's interface is localized in **21 languages**:

English, French, German, Spanish, Portuguese (Portugal), Portuguese (Brazil), Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Russian, Ukrainian, Italian, Dutch, Turkish, Polish, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, Finnish
UCS category names are translated from the official UCS v8.2.1 translations database.
